supply_chain.dependencies: Dependent on independent insurance agencies as sole distribution channel for the Exchange
supply_chain.risks: Disruption in independent agency relationships could impair ability to retain and attract business

regulatory_risks: State insurance regulations requiring approval of intercompany service agreements and material amendments
Insurance holding company system regulations requiring registration and disclosure of operations

Extensive regulatory supervision of property and casualty insurers including licensing, examination, rate setting, market conduct, policy forms, investment limitations, claims practices, mandated participation in involuntary markets and guaranty funds, reserve adequacy, insurer solvency, restrictions on underwriting standards, accounting standards, transactions between affiliates, risk management, cybersecurity and data privacy
Changes in applicable insurance laws, tax statutes, cyber, privacy, and other laws and regulations could adversely impact business
Changes in the way regulators administer laws, tax statutes, or regulations could adversely impact business
Risk of class action lawsuits and regulatory investigations and inquiries
Federal and state laws relating to privacy and security of nonpublic personal information

Compliance with complex and evolving laws and regulations including internal control over financial reporting, securities regulation, data privacy and protection, cybersecurity, taxation, immigration, wage-and-hour standards and employment and labor relations
New and evolving regulations regarding cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and ESG matters
